new_classes.definition_en: Piquette: a beverage obtained by fermenting water mixed with grape marc (pomace/skins/seeds/stems remaining after pressing grapes), classified under CN subheading 2206 00 10 within heading 2206 (other fermented beverages). Governed by additional note 9 to Chapter 22.

new_classes.label_en: Piquette fermentation process (fermentation of water with grape marc)
new_classes.definition_en: The process of fermenting water that has been mixed with grape marc (pomace), producing piquette. This process distinguishes piquette from other fermented beverages under heading 2206.

new_classes.label_en: Grape marc (pomace: skins, seeds and stems remaining after pressing grapes)
new_classes.label_de: Trester (Pressrückstand aus Weintrauben: Schalen, Kerne und Stiele)
new_classes.definition_en: The solid residue (skins, seeds, stems) remaining after pressing grapes to extract juice, used as the fermentation substrate in piquette production.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criterion — that piquette is a fermented beverage made from water mixed with grape marc — is captured by the new Piquette22060010 class (subclass of FermentedBeverage2206), its PiquetteFermentationProcess, and the GrapeMarc part. The link to additional note 9 of Chapter 22 is referenced in the definition but cannot be fully formalised as an OWL axiom without the text of that note; this reduces coverage to 0.75. The disjointness complement restriction against GrapeWineOrMarcDistillate2208 guards against confusion with distilled marc-based spirits. No container-volume or alcohol-content thresholds are specified in the legal text for this subheading, so none are modelled.
